error_message: "We couldn't send the email just now" shown to applicant but the actual Mailgun/SMTP error is swallowed — applicant + admin can't tell whether config is missing, domain unverified, rate-limited, or recipient rejected. Resume link is also unavailable in response when sendResumeLink throws.
root_cause: Both POST email paths caught sendResumeLink errors with logger+Sentry only and returned {success:true, requiresVerification:true, emailSent:false}. No emailError string. Client could only show a generic message. Additional root cause: 2 more stale /professional/ path refs (buildResumeLinkFromToken fallback at apply.astro:408 + Start New Application href at apply.astro:231) survived the rename fix in bug-004 — only the 9 fetch paths + 3 resume links + 1 resend-link were updated.
fix: Capture err.message into a local `emailError: string | undefined` in both POST email branches; return it in the response when emailSent is false. showVerifyEmailPanel(email, emailSent, emailError) renders the server message inline. Tests assert emailError string. Also fixed the 2 remaining stale /professional/ URLs in apply.astro (buildResumeLinkFromToken fallback + Start New Application href).

error_message: toRow() writes empty string `''` for hidden (visibleWhen=false) fields whose column is in columnMap. Sheet looks like the user submitted empty; in reality the field was gated off.
root_cause: validate() correctly skips both validation AND copy-through for hidden fields (values bag never holds them). toRow walked walkFields(schema) which yields ALL fields regardless of visibility, then wrote `serializeValue(undefined, field) = ''` for hidden columns.
fix: Phase I: toRow now early-continues when `field.visibleWhen && !field.visibleWhen(values)`. Regression test added in associateApply.test.ts (visibleWhen excludes listingDetails). 277/277 tests pass.

error_message:
EMAIL_RE in apply.ts uses [\s@] character class — accepts CR/LF in email payload (header-injection risk). Unified header-injection-safe regex /[^
@\s]+@[^
@\s]+\.[^
@\s]+$/ lives in src/lib/forms/validators.ts:emailNZ. apply.ts still imports its own copy.
root_cause: Three duplicated copies of email regex (apply.ts:35 + checkout-pm.ts:7 + checkout-am.ts:7). checkout-pm/am used CR/LF-safe class [\r\n@\s], apply.ts used weaker [\s@]. Diff not noticed when each route was refactored independently.
fix: Phase A: emailNZ exported from src/lib/forms/validators.ts with the safe class. Tests assert CR/LF payloads (`a@b.com\r\nBcc: v@y.com`) are rejected. Phase C: apply.ts validation migrates to use emailNZ, closing the bug at the call site.

error_message: Loading overlay stuck on /advanced/apply — spinner never dismissed
root_cause: IIFE at line 281 uses `advancedApplySchema` as a JS variable (line 307) but only `token` was in define:vars. Browser throws ReferenceError at line 307, IIFE aborts before overlay-hide code at line 1386.
fix: Added `advancedApplySchema` to define:vars: `<script is:inline define:vars={{ token, advancedApplySchema }}>`

error_message: "Network error." on Start Application button at /advanced/apply
root_cause: Page renamed professional→advanced but 9 fetch() calls in apply.astro still targeted /api/professional/* (404). Resume link URLs in api/advanced/apply.ts (3 occurrences) and resend-link.ts (1) still built /professional/apply?token= strings — broken email links.
fix: sed replaced all /api/professional/* → /api/advanced/* in apply.astro; replaced /professional/apply?token= → /advanced/apply?token= in both API handlers.
